scenario: A Web Modeler project contains multiple types of resources. The team is adding artifacts to support a new feature.
question: Which resource types can a Web Modeler project hold?

options.text: Running process instances in Camunda 8 continue to execute on the process definition version that was active when they were started. Deploying a new version does not migrate existing instances automatically; each instance retains its original version for its entire lifecycle unless an explicit process instance migration operation is performed. New instances started after deployment will use the latest version by default, ensuring backward compatibility for in-flight work.

Camunda 8 automatically migrates all running process instances to the latest deployed version upon each new deployment. Zeebe applies a rolling upgrade strategy that pauses active tokens, re-maps them to the equivalent elements in the new version using element IDs, and resumes execution. This ensures operational uniformity but may cause unexpected behaviour if the new version changes element names or restructures the flow significantly.

Deploying a new version in Camunda 8 terminates all active instances of the previous version and emits a version-change event that triggers compensating boundary events on all affected tokens before cancellation. This allows teams to clean up in-flight data but requires compensation handlers to be modelled on every task in the process to avoid data loss during forced migration upgrades.

When a new process version is deployed, Camunda 8 enters a dual-version mode where the broker simultaneously maintains two execution contexts. Both versions remain fully active, and the load balancer distributes new instances equally between them until the operator explicitly retires the older version through the Operate UI. This eliminates downtime but doubles resource consumption on the Zeebe partition cluster.

a.text: Correct. Per web-modeler/index.md:5,12 Web Modeler is "for modeling BPMN, DMN, Camunda Forms, and element templates." Connector templates are a subset of element templates. Additional Web Modeler features (Process applications, Git sync, IDP, Play, Marketplace per index.md:17-30) wrap these resources but aren't themselves distinct resource types.

b.text: Incorrect. Multi-artifact support is core to Web Modeler.

c.text: Incorrect. Forms and element templates are first-class in Web Modeler.

d.text: Incorrect. RPA scripts in 8.8 are authored in Desktop Modeler (per rpa/getting-started.md:22), not Web Modeler -- they are NOT a Web Modeler project resource type.

explanation: Per web-modeler/index.md:5,12 Web Modeler is "for modeling BPMN, DMN, Camunda Forms, and element templates." Connector templates are a subset of element templates. Additional Web Modeler features (Process applications, Git sync, IDP, Play, Marketplace per index.md:17-30) wrap these resources but aren't themselves distinct resource types.
